---
title: NY HP Rate Design Analysis

toc: true
reference-location: margin
fig-cap-location: bottom

appendix-style: default
citation-location: document
citation:
  container-title: Switchbox

format:
  html:
    page-layout: full

execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook analyzes CAIRO simulation outputs for New York heat pump rate
design. It produces two families of visualization:

1. **Bill change histograms** — weighted distributions of annual total energy
   bill changes after switching to heat pumps, by population subgroup.
2. **Median electric bill component breakdown** — stacked bar chart decomposing
   the weighted-median electric bill into supply, delivery volumetric, and
   delivery fixed charges for current HVAC vs heat pump scenarios.

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import cast

import polars as pl
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    geom_col,
    geom_text,
    geom_vline,
    ggplot,
    labs,
    position_stack,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_discrete,
    theme_minimal,
)

from lib.cairo import add_delivered_fuel_bills
from lib.data.eia.heating_fuel_prices import load_monthly_fuel_prices
from lib.data.nrel.resstock import scan_load_curves_for_utility
from lib.data.s3 import list_s3_subdirs, run_dir
from lib.rdp import fetch_rdp_file, parse_urdb_json
```

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params-top

UTILITY = "coned"
OUTPUT_BATCH = "20260305T211404Z"
STATE = "NY"
```

```{python}
#| label: params
state_lower = STATE.lower()
PRICE_YEAR = 2024

S3_BUCKET = "s3://data.sb"
PATH_RESSTOCK_BASE = f"{S3_BUCKET}/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2"
PATH_RESSTOCK_RELEASE = f"{PATH_RESSTOCK_BASE}_sb"
PATH_METADATA = f"{PATH_RESSTOCK_RELEASE}/metadata/state={STATE}/upgrade=00/metadata-sb.parquet"
PATH_HEATING_FUEL_PRICES = f"{S3_BUCKET}/eia/heating_fuel_prices/"

PATH_OUTPUT_BASE = (
    f"{S3_BUCKET}/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates/{state_lower}/{UTILITY}/{OUTPUT_BATCH}"
)

_run_dirs = list_s3_subdirs(PATH_OUTPUT_BASE)
_suffix = f"{state_lower}_{UTILITY}_run"
PATH_RUN_DELIVERY = run_dir(_run_dirs, f"{_suffix}1_up00_precalc__flat")
PATH_RUN_SUPPLY = run_dir(_run_dirs, f"{_suffix}2_up00_precalc_supply__flat")
PATH_RUN_DELIVERY_HP = run_dir(_run_dirs, f"{_suffix}3_up02_default__flat")
PATH_RUN_SUPPLY_HP = run_dir(_run_dirs, f"{_suffix}4_up02_default_supply__flat")

RDP_REF = "main"  # pin to a commit SHA or tag for reproducible runs
_tariff_flat = parse_urdb_json(
    fetch_rdp_file(
        f"rate_design/hp_rates/{state_lower}/config/tariffs/electric/{UTILITY}_flat.json",
        RDP_REF,
    )
)
FIXED_CHARGE_PER_MONTH = float(_tariff_flat["items"][0]["fixedchargefirstmeter"])
```

## Constants

```{python}
#| label: constants

ANNUAL_MONTH = "Annual"
BLDG_ID = "bldg_id"
BILL_LEVEL = "bill_level"
COMB_BILLS_CSV = "bills/comb_bills_year_target.csv"
ELEC_BILLS_CSV = "bills/elec_bills_year_run.csv"
HEATING_TYPE_COL = "postprocess_group.heating_type"
FOSSIL_FUEL = "fossil_fuel"
```

## Data ingest

```{python}
#| label: ingest

metadata = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_METADATA).collect()
monthly_prices = load_monthly_fuel_prices(PATH_HEATING_FUEL_PRICES, STATE, PRICE_YEAR)
```


## Bill change distributions

Weighted histograms of annual total energy bill change (before vs after switching
to heat pumps) under default rates. Combined bills are topped up with delivered
fuel (oil/propane) costs using monthly ResStock consumption and EIA prices.

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-deltas

comb_before = pl.scan_csv(f"{PATH_RUN_SUPPLY}/{COMB_BILLS_CSV}")
comb_after = pl.scan_csv(f"{PATH_RUN_SUPPLY_HP}/{COMB_BILLS_CSV}")

lc_monthly_u00 = scan_load_curves_for_utility(
    PATH_RESSTOCK_BASE, STATE, "00", UTILITY, "monthly"
)
lc_monthly_u02 = scan_load_curves_for_utility(
    PATH_RESSTOCK_BASE, STATE, "02", UTILITY, "monthly"
)

topped_before = add_delivered_fuel_bills(comb_before, lc_monthly_u00, monthly_prices)
topped_after = add_delivered_fuel_bills(comb_after, lc_monthly_u02, monthly_prices)

annual_before = (
    topped_before.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(pl.col(BLDG_ID), pl.col("weight"), pl.col(BILL_LEVEL).alias("bill_before"))
    .collect()
)
annual_after = (
    topped_after.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .select(pl.col(BLDG_ID), pl.col(BILL_LEVEL).alias("bill_after"))
    .collect()
)

delta_df = annual_before.join(annual_after, on=BLDG_ID, how="inner").with_columns(
    (pl.col("bill_after") - pl.col("bill_before")).alias("delta")
)

META_COLS = [
    BLDG_ID,
    "postprocess_group.has_hp",
    "postprocess_group.heating_type",
    "heats_with_natgas",
    "heats_with_oil",
    "heats_with_propane",
]
delta_with_meta = delta_df.join(metadata.select(META_COLS), on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
```

```{python}
#| label: histogram-helpers

POPULATION_FILTERS: dict[str, pl.Expr] = {
    "non_hp": pl.col("postprocess_group.has_hp") == False,
    "natgas": pl.col("heats_with_natgas") == True,
    "oil_propane": (pl.col("heats_with_oil") == True) | (pl.col("heats_with_propane") == True),
    "electric_resistance": pl.col("postprocess_group.heating_type") == "electrical_resistance",
}

BIN_WIDTH = 100
QUADRANT_COLORS = {
    "savings > $1k": "#1b5e20",
    "savings $0-1k": "#81c784",
    "losses $0-1k": "#ef9a9a",
    "losses > $1k": "#b71c1c",
}
QUADRANT_ORDER = list(QUADRANT_COLORS.keys())


def plot_bill_change_histogram(df: pl.DataFrame) -> ggplot:
    """Weighted histogram of annual bill change."""
    binned = (
        df.with_columns(
            (
                (pl.col("delta") / BIN_WIDTH).floor() * BIN_WIDTH + BIN_WIDTH / 2
            ).alias("bin_center"),
        )
        .with_columns(
            pl.when(pl.col("bin_center") <= -1000)
            .then(pl.lit("savings > $1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") > -1000) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 0))
            .then(pl.lit("savings $0-1k"))
            .when((pl.col("bin_center") >= 0) & (pl.col("bin_center") < 1000))
            .then(pl.lit("losses $0-1k"))
            .otherwise(pl.lit("losses > $1k"))
            .alias("quadrant"),
        )
        .group_by("bin_center", "quadrant")
        .agg(pl.col("weight").sum().alias("weight_sum"))
    )

    total_weight = df["weight"].sum()
    pct = {
        "savings > $1k": (
            df.filter(pl.col("delta") < -1000)["weight"].sum() / total_weight * 100
        ),
        "savings $0-1k": (
            df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= -1000) & (pl.col("delta") < 0))["weight"].sum()
            / total_weight * 100
        ),
        "losses $0-1k": (
            df.filter((pl.col("delta") >= 0) & (pl.col("delta") < 1000))["weight"].sum()
            / total_weight * 100
        ),
        "losses > $1k": (
            df.filter(pl.col("delta") >= 1000)["weight"].sum() / total_weight * 100
        ),
    }

    quadrant_dtype = pl.Enum(QUADRANT_ORDER)
    plot_df = binned.with_columns(pl.col("quadrant").cast(quadrant_dtype))
    max_weight = (
        cast(float, plot_df["weight_sum"].max()) if not plot_df.is_empty() else 0.0
    )
    y_annot = max_weight * 1.05

    return (
        ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="bin_center", y="weight_sum", fill="quadrant"))
        + geom_col(width=BIN_WIDTH * 0.9)
        + geom_vline(xintercept=[-1000, 0, 1000], linetype="dotted", color="gray")
        + scale_fill_manual(values=QUADRANT_COLORS, breaks=QUADRANT_ORDER)
        + annotate(
            "text", x=-2500, y=y_annot,
            label=f"savings > $1k\n{pct['savings > $1k']:.1f}%",
            ha="center", size=9,
        )
        + annotate(
            "text", x=-500, y=y_annot,
            label=f"savings $0-1k\n{pct['savings $0-1k']:.1f}%",
            ha="center", size=9,
        )
        + annotate(
            "text", x=500, y=y_annot,
            label=f"losses $0-1k\n{pct['losses $0-1k']:.1f}%",
            ha="center", size=9,
        )
        + annotate(
            "text", x=2500, y=y_annot,
            label=f"losses > $1k\n{pct['losses > $1k']:.1f}%",
            ha="center", size=9,
        )
        + labs(
            x="Annual Bill Change ($)",
            y="# of Homes",
            title="Change in Total Energy Bills after Switching to Heat Pumps",
        )
        + theme_minimal()
    )
```

### Non-HP households

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-non-hp
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — non-heat-pump households"

filtered = delta_with_meta.filter(POPULATION_FILTERS["non_hp"]).select("delta", "weight")
assert not filtered.is_empty(), "No rows after non_hp filter"
plot_bill_change_histogram(filtered)
```

### Natural gas heated

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-natgas
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — natural gas-heated households"

filtered = delta_with_meta.filter(POPULATION_FILTERS["natgas"]).select("delta", "weight")
assert not filtered.is_empty(), "No rows after natgas filter"
plot_bill_change_histogram(filtered)
```

### Oil / propane heated

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-oil-propane
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — oil/propane-heated households"

filtered = delta_with_meta.filter(POPULATION_FILTERS["oil_propane"]).select("delta", "weight")
assert not filtered.is_empty(), "No rows after oil_propane filter"
plot_bill_change_histogram(filtered)
```

### Electric resistance heated

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-change-elec-resistance
#| fig-cap: "Annual bill change distribution — electric resistance-heated households"

filtered = delta_with_meta.filter(POPULATION_FILTERS["electric_resistance"]).select("delta", "weight")
assert not filtered.is_empty(), "No rows after electric_resistance filter"
plot_bill_change_histogram(filtered)
```


## Median electric bill components

Stacked bar chart of median electric bill components (supply / delivery
volumetric / delivery fixed) for the weighted-median fossil-fuel-heated customer
under current HVAC vs heat pump scenarios.

```{python}
#| label: compute-bill-components

def join_bills_and_compute_components(
    delivery_bills: pl.LazyFrame,
    supply_bills: pl.LazyFrame,
    annual_fixed_delivery: float,
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Join delivery + supply bills and decompose into supply / delivery vol / delivery fixed."""
    delivery_df = delivery_bills.collect()
    supply_df = (
        supply_bills.select(
            pl.col(BLDG_ID),
            pl.col("month"),
            pl.col(BILL_LEVEL).alias("bill_supply"),
            pl.col("weight").alias("weight_supply"),
        ).collect()
    )
    joined = delivery_df.join(supply_df, on=[BLDG_ID, "month"], how="inner")

    n_left = delivery_df.select(BLDG_ID, "month").unique().height
    n_joined = joined.height
    assert n_left == n_joined, (
        f"Join dropped rows: left had {n_left}, joined has {n_joined}"
    )
    weight_diff = (joined["weight"] - joined["weight_supply"]).abs()
    assert (weight_diff > 1e-9).sum() == 0, "Weight mismatch between delivery and supply"

    return joined.with_columns(
        (pl.col("bill_supply") - pl.col(BILL_LEVEL)).alias("supply"),
        pl.lit(annual_fixed_delivery).alias("delivery_fixed"),
        (pl.col(BILL_LEVEL) - annual_fixed_delivery).alias("delivery_volumetric"),
        pl.col("bill_supply").alias("total"),
    )


def weighted_median_row(
    df: pl.DataFrame,
    sort_col: str = "total",
    weight_col: str = "weight",
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Return the single row at the weighted median by sort_col."""
    total_weight = df[weight_col].sum()
    sorted_df = df.sort(sort_col)
    cum = sorted_df[weight_col].cum_sum()
    idx = (cum >= 0.5 * total_weight).arg_max()
    assert idx is not None, "Empty data or non-positive weights"
    return sorted_df.slice(idx, 1)


annual_fixed = FIXED_CHARGE_PER_MONTH * 12

SELECT_COLS = [BLDG_ID, "weight", "supply", "delivery_volumetric", "delivery_fixed", "total"]

# Current HVAC (u00): median fossil-fuel customer
components_current = join_bills_and_compute_components(
    pl.scan_csv(f"{PATH_RUN_DELIVERY}/{ELEC_BILLS_CSV}"),
    pl.scan_csv(f"{PATH_RUN_SUPPLY}/{ELEC_BILLS_CSV}"),
    annual_fixed,
)
fossil_current = (
    components_current.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .join(metadata.select(BLDG_ID, HEATING_TYPE_COL), on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == FOSSIL_FUEL)
)
assert not fossil_current.is_empty(), "No fossil-fuel buildings in current-HVAC scenario"
median_current = weighted_median_row(fossil_current.select(SELECT_COLS))

# Heat pump (u02): median fossil-fuel customer
components_hp = join_bills_and_compute_components(
    pl.scan_csv(f"{PATH_RUN_DELIVERY_HP}/{ELEC_BILLS_CSV}"),
    pl.scan_csv(f"{PATH_RUN_SUPPLY_HP}/{ELEC_BILLS_CSV}"),
    annual_fixed,
)
fossil_hp = (
    components_hp.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
    .join(metadata.select(BLDG_ID, HEATING_TYPE_COL), on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
    .filter(pl.col(HEATING_TYPE_COL) == FOSSIL_FUEL)
)
assert not fossil_hp.is_empty(), "No fossil-fuel buildings in HP scenario"
median_hp = weighted_median_row(fossil_hp.select(SELECT_COLS))
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-bill-components-stacked
#| fig-cap: "Median electric bill components: current HVAC vs mid-efficiency heat pump"

COMPONENT_NAMES: dict[str, str] = {
    "supply": "Supply Charge",
    "delivery_fixed": "Delivery Charge (Fixed)",
    "delivery_volumetric": "Delivery Charge (Volumetric)",
}
SCENARIO_ORDER = ["Current HVAC", "Air-source heat pump\n(mid-efficiency)"]
COMPONENT_ORDER = [
    "Delivery Charge (Fixed)",
    "Delivery Charge (Volumetric)",
    "Supply Charge",
]


def unpivot_median(median_df: pl.DataFrame, scenario: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Unpivot a single-row median into long-form (scenario, component, value)."""
    cols = list(COMPONENT_NAMES.keys())
    return (
        median_df.select(cols)
        .unpivot(on=cols, variable_name="col", value_name="value")
        .with_columns(
            pl.col("col").replace_strict(COMPONENT_NAMES).alias("component"),
            pl.lit(scenario).alias("scenario"),
        )
        .drop("col")
    )


long = pl.concat([
    unpivot_median(median_current, SCENARIO_ORDER[0]),
    unpivot_median(median_hp, SCENARIO_ORDER[1]),
])

scenario_dtype = pl.Enum(SCENARIO_ORDER)
component_dtype = pl.Enum(COMPONENT_ORDER)

plot_df = long.with_columns(
    pl.col("scenario").cast(scenario_dtype),
    pl.col("component").cast(component_dtype),
    pl.col("value")
    .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}", return_dtype=pl.String)
    .alias("label"),
)

totals = (
    plot_df.group_by("scenario")
    .agg(pl.col("value").sum())
    .with_columns(
        pl.col("value")
        .map_elements(lambda v: f"${v:,.0f}", return_dtype=pl.String)
        .alias("label"),
    )
)

(
    ggplot(plot_df, aes(x="scenario", y="value", fill="component"))
    + geom_col(position=position_stack(reverse=True), width=0.6)
    + geom_text(
        aes(label="label"),
        position=position_stack(vjust=0.5, reverse=True),
        color="white", fontweight="bold", size=11,
    )
    + geom_text(
        aes(x="scenario", y="value", label="label"),
        data=totals,
        va="bottom", color="#333333", fontweight="bold",
        size=11, nudge_y=30, inherit_aes=False,
    )
    + scale_fill_manual(
        values={
            "Supply Charge": "#E69F00",
            "Delivery Charge (Fixed)": "#01796F",
            "Delivery Charge (Volumetric)": "#56B4E9",
        },
    )
    + scale_x_discrete(limits=SCENARIO_ORDER)
    + labs(
        x="",
        y="Annual electric bill ($)",
        title="Median electric bill components: current HVAC vs mid-efficiency heat pump",
    )
    + theme_minimal()
)
```
